content: 2024-2025 SOTA active learning agent memory systems can be reframed as hierarchical curiosity filters, where each tier of long-term memory prioritizes retaining unexplored, uncertainty-reducing experience traces over frequently repeated familiar ones, analogous to how a core agent’s intrinsic curiosity drive shapes action selection. This design flips the standard memory prioritization logic: instead of rewarding recall frequency, it rewards the future information gain that stored memory traces can unlock for downstream active learning rounds.
